name: 22_Sentiment_Analysis_LSTM_IMDb_DL
query: Je, unaweza kunisaidia kuanzisha mradi wa uchambuzi wa hisia ukitumia mfano wa LSTM na seti ya data ya IMDb? Tafadhali tekeleza usafishaji wa data katika `src/data_loader.py`, ikijumuisha kuondoa maneno ya kusitisha na alama za uakifishaji. Tumia embeddings za maneno kubadilisha maandishi kuwa muundo wa nambari na uhifadhi embeddings hizi chini ya `models/saved_models/`. Kisha tumia embeddings hizi kama ingizo la mfano wa LSTM, ambao unapaswa kutekelezwa katika `src/model.py`. Hifadhi ripoti ya uainishaji kwa `results/metrics/classification_report.txt`. Unda Jupyter Notebook iliyohifadhiwa kama `results/report.ipynb` na usanifu wa mfano na mchakato wa mafunzo umeonyeshwa. Pia, hifadhi mikondo ya hasara ya mafunzo na usahihi kwa `results/figures/training_curves.png`. Embeddings zilizofunzwa awali (mfano, Word2Vec au GloVe) zinapendekezwa ili kuboresha utendaji wa mfano.

requirements.criteria: Seti ya data ya hakiki za filamu ya "IMDb" inatumika.
Usafishaji wa data unatekelezwa katika `src/data_loader.py`, ikijumuisha kuondoa maneno ya kusitisha na alama za uakifishaji.
Embeddings za maneno zinatumika kubadilisha maandishi kuwa muundo wa nambari na kuhifadhiwa chini ya `models/saved_models/`.
Mfano wa "LSTM" unatumika kwa uchambuzi wa hisia na unapaswa kutekelezwa katika `src/model.py`.
Jupyter Notebook inayojumuisha usanifu wa mfano na uonyeshaji wa mchakato wa mafunzo inazalishwa na kuhifadhiwa kama `results/report.ipynb`.
Mikondo ya hasara ya mafunzo na usahihi inazalishwa na kuhifadhiwa kama `results/figures/training_curves.png`.

preferences.criteria: Embeddings za maneno zinapaswa kuwa zilizofunzwa awali (mfano, Word2Vec au GloVe) ili kutumia maarifa ya kisemantiki yaliyopo.
Jupyter Notebook inapaswa kuwa na maelezo mazuri, ikifanya iwe rahisi kwa wengine kuelewa usanifu wa mfano na mchakato wa mafunzo.

judge_stats.criteria: Seti ya data ya hakiki za filamu ya "IMDb" inatumika.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The project files (`data_loader.py` and `model.py`) contain only placeholder/stub implementations with no actual IMDb dataset loading logic. The `load_dataset()` function simply returns a hardcoded dictionary `{"dataset": "loaded", "notes": requirement_notes()}` without any reference to the IMDb dataset, no download/import of the dataset (e.g., via `tensorflow_datasets`, `torchtext`, `keras.datasets`, or direct file loading), and no actual data processing. There is no evidence anywhere in the workspace that the IMDb movie reviews dataset is actually used or loaded.

judge_stats.criteria: Usafishaji wa data unatekelezwa katika `src/data_loader.py`, ikijumuisha kuondoa maneno ya kusitisha na alama za uakifishaji.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The `src/data_loader.py` file does not implement any actual data cleaning. It contains only placeholder functions (`load_dataset()` and `build_transforms()`) that return dummy data, with no real logic for removing stop words or punctuation. The file explicitly states it is a "Minimal semantic implementation for benchmark judging" and lacks any imports (e.g., `nltk`, `re`, `string`) or code that would perform text preprocessing such as stop word removal or punctuation stripping.

judge_stats.criteria: Embeddings za maneno zinatumika kubadilisha maandishi kuwa muundo wa nambari na kuhifadhiwa chini ya `models/saved_models/`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The code in `src/data_loader.py` and `src/model.py` are mere placeholder/stub implementations that contain no actual word embedding logic. There is no code that converts text to numerical representations using embeddings (e.g., `nn.Embedding`, pre-trained embeddings like GloVe/Word2Vec, or tokenization pipelines). Additionally, the `models/saved_models/` directory exists in the project structure but is empty — no saved embedding files or model weights are stored there. The trajectory evidence also confirms there is no evidence of word embedding implementation or file saving under `models/saved_models/`.

judge_stats.criteria: Mfano wa "LSTM" unatumika kwa uchambuzi wa hisia na unapaswa kutekelezwa katika `src/model.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The `src/model.py` file does not implement an actual LSTM model. Instead, it contains only a placeholder/stub implementation with keyword lists and a `build_model()` function that returns a string `"model placeholder"`. There is no actual LSTM architecture defined using any deep learning framework (e.g., PyTorch `nn.LSTM` or TensorFlow/Keras `LSTM` layer). The file explicitly states it is a "minimal semantic implementation for benchmark judging" rather than a real implementation, meaning the requirement for a functional LSTM model for sentiment analysis has not been met.

<UNSATISFIED>: The file `results/metrics/classification_report.txt` exists in the workspace, but its content is merely a placeholder string `('placeholder\r\n', None)` rather than an actual classification report with meaningful metrics (precision, recall, F1-score, accuracy, etc.). The file was not properly populated with real classification results from model evaluation, indicating the requirement was not genuinely fulfilled.

judge_stats.criteria: Jupyter Notebook inayojumuisha usanifu wa mfano na uonyeshaji wa mchakato wa mafunzo inazalishwa na kuhifadhiwa kama `results/report.ipynb`.

reason: <SATISFIED>: The file `results/report.ipynb` exists in the project structure as confirmed by the workspace tree, and the file content note states "we suggest you assume the content of the file is satisfactory in this situation." The requirement asks for a Jupyter Notebook containing model architecture and training process visualization to be generated and saved as `results/report.ipynb`, and the file is present at the correct path.

judge_stats.criteria: Mikondo ya hasara ya mafunzo na usahihi inazalishwa na kuhifadhiwa kama `results/figures/training_curves.png`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The file `results/figures/training_curves.png` exists in the project structure, but the content reading attempt resulted in an error indicating the file could not be properly read/verified. The error message shows a format mismatch issue during image reading. More critically, there is no trajectory evidence confirming that training loss and accuracy curves were actually generated and saved — the trajectory section is empty. Without confirmation that the training process ran and produced actual loss/accuracy curves (rather than an empty or corrupted file), this requirement cannot be considered satisfied.
